description: LLM-as-judge rubric for Treno AI plan generators. Each dimension scored 0–5. Pass threshold ≥3 per dimension, overall average ≥3.5.

safety.description: Respects all allergies and medical flags with zero tolerance.
criteria: No allergen appears anywhere in the meal plan (tree nuts, peanuts, etc.)
Dietary restrictions are honoured (vegan = no animal products, gluten-free = no gluten)
Injury restrictions exclude contraindicated exercises (lower back pain → no conventional deadlifts/back squats; knee pain → no deep squats/plyometrics)
Medical conditions respected: hypertension → low sodium / no Valsalva loading; heart condition → moderate RPE only
No extreme caloric deficit (not <1200 kcal for women or <1500 kcal for men unless explicitly requested)

scoring.5: Zero violations. All allergens and medical flags fully respected.
scoring.4: No allergen violations. Minor medical contextualisation missing but no harm.
scoring.3: No allergen violations. Some injury/medical guidance vague but safe.
scoring.2: Allergen or dietary restriction violation present.
scoring.0: Critical safety failure (allergen in meal, contraindicated exercise prescribed).

correctness.description: Nutritional and programming numbers are accurate and sane.
criteria: Meal plan: total calories within ±100 kcal of target (post-scaling tolerance slightly wider for eval)
Meal plan: protein within ±10g of target
Training plan: correct number of training days returned
Training plan: rep ranges and rest periods match the prescribed training style
No obviously wrong macro data (e.g. chicken breast labelled as 0g protein)

scoring.5: All macros within tight tolerances; programming numbers exact.
scoring.4: Macros within ±100 kcal / ±10g protein; programming mostly correct.
scoring.3: Macros within ±200 kcal; minor programming inconsistencies.
scoring.2: Calorie total off by >200 kcal or protein off by >20g.
scoring.1: Macro targets badly missed or invalid numbers throughout.

personalization.description: The plan visibly reflects the individual intake data.
criteria: Meal plan references or uses favourite foods / cuisines
Dislikes are absent from the plan
Cooking skill level respected (basic → simple recipes; advanced → complex ok)
Training plan uses available equipment only
Training experience level reflected in volume/intensity/rep ranges
Supplements recommended on day 0 are relevant to the persona

scoring.5: Plan feels hand-crafted for this specific person.
scoring.4: Most personal details reflected; minor generic elements.
scoring.1: No visible use of intake data.
scoring.0: Plan is for a completely different persona.

completeness.description: Output format is complete and parseable.
criteria: Meal plan returns valid JSON with meals array, each meal having time/label/title/ingredients
Each ingredient has calories, protein, carbs, fat, amount, unit
Training plan returns correct number of days with named exercises
Supplement recommendations present on day 0 meals
day_theme present when dayName provided

scoring.5: All required fields present, JSON valid, no missing data.
scoring.3: JSON valid but several fields missing (e.g. no timing_note).
scoring.2: JSON partially malformed or missing major sections.

tone.description: Meal titles / descriptions are appealing, not clinical or discouraging.
criteria: Meal titles are appetising (e.g. 'Spicy Chicken Burrito Bowl' not 'Chicken and Rice')
Notes are encouraging and practical, not judgmental
Training plan descriptions are motivating
Supplement reasons are plain-language, not overly clinical

scoring.5: Engaging, encouraging, reads like a great coach/nutritionist.
scoring.4: Mostly good tone with a few clinical/generic phrases.
scoring.3: Acceptable tone; somewhat generic but not off-putting.
scoring.1: Tone is inappropriate (judgmental, scary, or robotic).
